---
title: "Reference Sheet"
output: html_document
---

This cheat sheet is provided during the programming assessment. It contains the syntax you have learned in class.

```{r setp, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
```

---

# Creating variables

```{r}
x <- 5
y <- 10
z <- "Hello"
```

---

# Vectors

```{r}
x <- c(1, 2, 3)

x[1]      # first value
x[2]      # second value
```

---

# Comparison operators

```{r}
x > 5
x >= 5
x < 5
x <= 5
x == 5
x != 5
```

---

# Logical operators

```{r}
TRUE & TRUE     # AND
TRUE | FALSE    # OR
!TRUE           # NOT
```

---

# Logical vectors

```{r}
x <- c(1, 2, 3)

x > 1
```

---

# Vector subsetting

```{r}
x <- c(1, 2, 3)

y <- x > 1

x[y]
```

---

# Useful functions

```{r}
x <- c(1, 2, 3)

sum(x)
mean(x)
length(x)
```

```{r}
x <- c(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3)

names(x)
```

---

# If statements

```{r}
if (TRUE) {
  # ...
} else {
  # ...
}
```

---

# For loops

Loop over values:

```{r}
for (val in x) {
  ...
}
```

Loop over names:

```{r}
for (id in names(x)) {
  ...
}
```

---

# Accessing values by name

```{r}
x <- c(a = 1, b = 2, c = 3)

x["a"]
```
